subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Two Particles on x-axis — Velocity, Opposite Directions & Position
stimulus: Two particles, $H$ and $J$, are moving along the $x$-axis. For $0 \le t \le 5$, the position of particle $H$ at time $t$ is given by $x_H(t) = e^{t^2 - 4t}$ and the velocity of particle $J$ at time $t$ is given by $v_J(t) = 2t(t^2-1)^3$.

parts.prompt: Find the velocity of particle $H$ at time $t = 1$. Show the work that leads to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: P1 earned for $x_H'(t)$, $x_H'(1)$, or $(2t-4)e^{t^2-4t}$ or $(2\cdot1-4)e^{1-4}$. P2 earned for $-2e^{-3}$ or equivalent. An unsupported answer of $-2e^{-3}$ earns P2 but not P1.

parts.prompt: During what open intervals of time $t$, for $0 < t < 5$, are particles $H$ and $J$ moving in opposite directions? Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Considers signs of $v_H(t)$ or $v_J(t)$: $v_H(t) = (2t-4)e^{t^2-4t} = 0 \Rightarrow t = 2$; $v_J(t) = 2t(t^2-1)^3 = 0 \Rightarrow t = 1$ for $0 < t < 5$
1 pt: Analyzes direction for one particle: $v_H < 0$ for $0 < t < 2$, $v_H > 0$ for $2 < t < 5$; or $v_J < 0$ for $0 < t < 1$, $v_J > 0$ for $1 < t < 5$
1 pt: Correct answer with complete reasoning: particles move in opposite directions for $1 < t < 2$ (H moves left, J moves right)
parts.scoring_notes: Particle H: $v_H < 0$ for $0 < t < 2$ (moving left), $v_H > 0$ for $2 < t < 5$ (moving right). Particle J: $v_J < 0$ for $0 < t < 1$ (moving left), $v_J > 0$ for $1 < t < 5$ (moving right). Opposite directions only when one is positive and the other negative: $1 < t < 2$. Must analyze both particles for P5.

parts.prompt: It can be shown that $v_J'(2) > 0$. Is the speed of particle $J$ increasing, decreasing, or neither at time $t = 2$? Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Speed is increasing at $t = 2$ because $v_J(2) = 2(2)(3)^3 = 108 > 0$ and $v_J'(2) > 0$ (given), so velocity and acceleration have the same sign
parts.scoring_notes: Since $v_J(2) = 108 > 0$ and $v_J'(2) > 0$ (same sign), speed is increasing. Can import sign of $v_J(2)$ from part (b). An evaluation of $v_J(2)$ is not required; stating $v_J(2) > 0$ (consistent with part b analysis) suffices.

parts.prompt: Particle $J$ is at position $x = 7$ at time $t = 0$. Find the position of particle $J$ at time $t = 2$. Show the work that leads to your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integrand: $\int_0^2 v_J(t)\, dt = \int_0^2 2t(t^2-1)^3\, dt$
1 pt: Correct antiderivative: $\frac{1}{4}(t^2-1)^4 + C$ (using $u = t^2-1$, $du = 2t\, dt$)
